info.name: Order Management System
info.description:
Complete API collection for the Order Management System.

Architecture: Temporal Workflows + Event Sourcing + Hexagonal Architecture

## Quick Start
1. Import this collection
2. Run 'Happy Path' folder in sequence

## Ports
- Order Service:      http://localhost:8080
- Inventory Service:  http://localhost:8081
- Payment Service:    http://localhost:8082
- Shipping Service:   http://localhost:8083
- Kong Gateway:       http://localhost:8000

## UIs
- Swagger:  http://localhost:8080/swagger-ui.html
- Temporal: http://localhost:8088
- Kafka UI: http://localhost:9000
- Jaeger:   http://localhost:16686

## Seeded Products (use these UUIDs for productId)
- a0eebc99-9c0b-4ef8-bb6d-6bb9bd380a11  Laptop Pro 15in        ($1,199.99)
- a0eebc99-9c0b-4ef8-bb6d-6bb9bd380a12  Wireless Mouse         ($59.99)
- a0eebc99-9c0b-4ef8-bb6d-6bb9bd380a13  USB-C Hub              ($49.99)
- a0eebc99-9c0b-4ef8-bb6d-6bb9bd380a14  Mechanical Keyboard    ($199.99)
- a0eebc99-9c0b-4ef8-bb6d-6bb9bd380a15  Monitor 27in           ($799.99)

## Simulated Failure Rates
- Inventory: 20% (configured in inventory-service application.yml)
- Payment:   30% (configured in payment-service application.yml)
- Shipping:  10% (configured in shipping-service application.yml)
Temporal retries handle transient failures automatically.

item.name: 🚀 Happy Path (Run in Sequence)
item.description:
Full order lifecycle: Create → Add Items → Confirm → Watch workflow complete.

Items added: 1× Mechanical Keyboard + 2× USB-C Hub = $299.97 total.
The Temporal saga runs: Reserve Inventory → Process Payment → Create Shipment → Confirm Delivery.
Payment has a 30% simulated failure rate — Temporal retries automatically (maxAttempts=3).

request.description: Creates an order in DRAFT status. Raises OrderCreatedEvent (v1).

Raises ItemAddedEvent (v2). Only valid in DRAFT status.
productId must match a seeded inventory_items row — inventory-service validates on reserve.
State is rebuilt by replaying events from the event store — no direct state read.

Transitions DRAFT → CONFIRMED and launches Temporal saga:
Reserve Inventory → Process Payment → Create Shipment → Confirm Delivery

Returns 202 immediately; poll GET /orders/{id} to watch progress.

exec: console.log('Order status:', json.status, '| Payment:', json.paymentStatus, '| Shipment:', json.shipmentStatus, '| Tracking:', json.trackingNumber);

request.description:
Run repeatedly to watch saga progress: CONFIRMED → SHIPPED → DELIVERED.
Payment retries happen automatically inside Temporal (30% failure rate, maxAttempts=3).
Human-readable event history. Shows every state transition with timestamp.
Returns raw domain event records from the event_store table.

exec: console.log('Workflow:', json.status, '| Step:', json.currentStep, '| Retries:', json.retryCount);

request.description:
Reads LIVE in-memory workflow state via Temporal Query — no DB hit, no event replay.
Returns UNKNOWN once the workflow completes (Temporal no longer holds it in memory).

item.name: ❌ Saga Compensation (Cancel Flow)
item.description:
Demonstrates saga rollback. Create → Add Item → Confirm → Cancel.
Watch compensation: InventoryReleased → (RefundPayment if charged) → OrderCancelled.

exec: console.log('Cancel sent. Check timeline to see compensation events.');

request.description: Sends CancelOrder signal to Temporal. Compensation: ReleaseInventory → (RefundPayment if charged) → OrderCancelled.
Should show InventoryReleasedEvent and OrderCancelledEvent.

item.name: 🔄 Order Service — All Endpoints
item.description: Individual requests for every Order and Workflow controller endpoint.

request.description:
Removes item from order. Only valid in DRAFT status. Raises ItemRemovedEvent.
productId in the path must match an item already added to the order.
Transitions DRAFT → CONFIRMED. Starts Temporal fulfillment workflow. Returns 202 with workflowId.
Sends cancel signal to Temporal workflow or directly cancels a DRAFT order.
Records a payment event on the order. Called by the payment activity in the workflow.
Sends RetryPayment signal to the Temporal workflow. Returns 202.
Returns current order state reconstructed from event replay.
Returns raw domain event records from the event_store table.
Human-readable event history with descriptions and timestamps.

item.name: ⚙️ Workflow Controller

exec: console.log('Status:', json.status, '| Step:', json.currentStep, '| Retries:', json.retryCount, '| CancelRequested:', json.cancellationRequested);

request.description:
Reads live in-memory workflow state via Temporal Query — no DB hit, no event replay.

Possible currentStep values:
- RESERVING_INVENTORY  — inside reserveInventory activity (10s simulated delay)
- PAYMENT_PENDING      — 10s cancel window BEFORE payment is scheduled. Send cancel here to prevent any charge.
- PROCESSING_PAYMENT   — inside processPayment activity (10s simulated delay)
- WAITING_FOR_PAYMENT_RETRY — paused up to 5 min waiting for RetryPayment signal
- SHIPMENT_PENDING     — 10s cancel window BEFORE shipment is scheduled. Send cancel here; payment will be refunded.
- CREATING_SHIPMENT    — inside createShipment activity
- AWAITING_DELIVERY    — waiting for delivery confirmation
- COMPLETED            — saga finished successfully
- CANCELLED            — order cancelled (compensation completed)
- PAYMENT_FAILED       — payment exhausted all retries, saga compensated
- INVENTORY_FAILED     — inventory unavailable, order cancelled

Returns UNKNOWN status if workflow has already completed (Temporal releases in-memory state on completion).

Sends a CancelOrder signal directly to the Temporal workflow, bypassing domain validation.

Difference from POST /orders/{id}/cancel:
- POST /orders/{id}/cancel   — goes through the Order aggregate (validates state, may reject)
- POST /workflows/{id}/signal/cancel — sends signal straight to Temporal (ops use, always delivered)

The signal is queued and processed at the next cancel checkpoint:
- During PAYMENT_PENDING (10s window)  → inventory released, payment NEVER charged
- During PROCESSING_PAYMENT activity   → payment completes, then refunded by compensation
- During SHIPMENT_PENDING (10s window) → payment refunded, shipment NEVER created

Signal is silently ignored if the workflow has already completed.

Wakes a workflow paused at WAITING_FOR_PAYMENT_RETRY state.

After a transient payment failure (network timeout, not InsufficientFunds), the workflow calls:
  Workflow.await(5 minutes, () -> retryRequested)
and waits up to 5 minutes for this signal instead of immediately compensating.

This signal sets retryRequested=true, causing the workflow to re-attempt processPayment.
Has no effect if the workflow is not in WAITING_FOR_PAYMENT_RETRY state.

If the 5-minute window expires without this signal, the workflow auto-cancels: ReleaseInventory → OrderCancelled.

item.name: 📦 Inventory Service (Direct)
item.description:
Inventory CRUD management + saga reservation endpoints.

CRUD endpoints let you manage the product catalogue at runtime.
Reservation endpoints are called by order-service InventoryActivity during the Temporal saga.

Seeded products (UUID keys, use for order items):
- a0eebc99-...-a11  Laptop Pro 15in
- a0eebc99-...-a12  Wireless Mouse
- a0eebc99-...-a13  USB-C Hub
- a0eebc99-...-a14  Mechanical Keyboard
- a0eebc99-...-a15  Monitor 27in

exec: json.forEach(p => console.log(p.productId, '|', p.productName, '| available:', p.quantityAvailable, '| reserved:', p.quantityReserved));

request.description:
Returns all products with current stock levels (quantityAvailable + quantityReserved).
quantityReserved reflects active Temporal saga reservations.

Get a single product by productId. Returns 404 if not found.
Default product_id variable is PROD-001 (Laptop Pro 15").

Creates a new product in the inventory catalogue.

productId: any unique string (PROD-xxx or UUID — use UUID format if you want order-service to reserve it via Temporal).
quantityAvailable: initial stock (must be >= 0).
quantityReserved starts at 0.

Returns 409 Conflict if productId already exists.

exec: console.log('Updated:', json.productName, '| available:', json.quantityAvailable, '| reserved:', json.quantityReserved);

request.description:
Partial update — only include fields you want to change.

quantityAvailable: set new stock level (null = keep existing).
productName: rename the product (null = keep existing).
quantityReserved is managed by the saga and cannot be set here.

Returns 404 if productId not found.
Rename only — quantityAvailable is omitted so it stays unchanged.

Permanently deletes a product from the catalogue.
Do not delete products that have active reservations — the release compensation will fail.
Returns 204 No Content on success, 404 if not found.

Idempotent: same orderId always returns the same reservationId.
20% simulated failure rate — retried automatically by Temporal InventoryActivity.
Releases a reservation. Saga compensation step called by releaseInventory activity.

item.name: 💳 Payment Service (Direct)
item.description:
Internal endpoints — called by order-service PaymentActivity during the Temporal saga.
Bypasses Kong. Useful for manual debugging or simulating partial saga state.

request.description:
Idempotent: same orderId always returns same result.
30% simulated failure rate — retried automatically by Temporal PaymentActivity.
Refunds a charge. Saga compensation step called by refundPayment activity.

item.name: 🚚 Shipping Service (Direct)
item.description:
Internal endpoints — called by order-service ShippingActivity during the Temporal saga.
Bypasses Kong. Useful for manual debugging or simulating partial saga state.

exec: console.log('Shipment:', json.shipmentId, '| Carrier:', json.carrier, '| Tracking:', json.trackingNumber);

request.description:
Idempotent: same orderId returns same shipmentId.
10% simulated failure rate — retried automatically by Temporal ShippingActivity.

Simulates carrier webhook confirming delivery. Sets shipment status to DELIVERED.
shipment_id is set automatically by the 'Create Shipment' test script above.

item.name: 🔥 Failure Scenarios
item.description:
Test saga compensation paths and cancel windows.

Scenario A — Cancel Before Payment: cancel during PAYMENT_PENDING window → no charge ever made.
Scenario B — Payment failure + retry signal: transient failure, customer retries payment.
Scenario C — Force payment failure: set failure rate to 100%, all retries fail, saga compensates.
Scenario D — Cancel mid-flight: cancel arrives while payment/shipment activity is running → refund issued.

exec: console.log('Now poll A4 until currentStep=PAYMENT_PENDING, then immediately send A5 cancel.');

request.description:
Starts the Temporal saga. The workflow will:
1. awaitCancelWindow(10s) — pre-inventory cancel window
2. Run reserveInventory (~10s)
3. Enter PAYMENT_PENDING — 10s cancel window before payment

After running this request, poll A4 every second until currentStep=PAYMENT_PENDING.
You have 10 seconds to send A5 before payment is scheduled.

exec: console.log('>>> PAYMENT_PENDING window is OPEN. Run A5 cancel NOW (you have 10 seconds).');
console.log('>>> Missed the window. Payment is running. Cancel will trigger refund (Scenario D).');

request.description:
Run this repeatedly (every ~1s) after A3 confirm.
When currentStep=PAYMENT_PENDING the 10-second cancel window is open.
Send A5 immediately — the window closes 10 seconds after PAYMENT_PENDING appears.

Expected sequence: RESERVING_INVENTORY (20s) → PAYMENT_PENDING (10s window) → PROCESSING_PAYMENT

exec: console.log('Cancel signal sent. Check A6 to verify paymentStatus=PENDING (never charged).');

request.description:
Send this while A4 shows currentStep=PAYMENT_PENDING.

Expected result (check A6):
- status: CANCELLED
- paymentStatus: PENDING   ← payment was NEVER charged (caught before activity scheduled)
- shipmentStatus: NOT_CREATED

If paymentStatus=COMPLETED: the 10-second window had already closed when you sent the cancel.
The payment ran and was refunded by compensation — this is Scenario D.

exec: console.log('SUCCESS: Cancel-before-payment worked. Payment was never charged.');
console.log('LATE CANCEL: Payment ran and was refunded. Missed the PAYMENT_PENDING window.');

request.description:
Verify the cancel-before-payment result.

paymentStatus=PENDING  → SUCCESS: cancel caught in PAYMENT_PENDING window, no charge ever made.
paymentStatus=REFUNDED → LATE: cancel arrived after payment ran, compensation issued a refund.

Check the event timeline (GET /orders/{id}/timeline) to see exactly which compensation steps ran.

item.name: B. Retry Payment Signal (after transient failure)

request.description:
After a transient payment failure the workflow enters WAITING_FOR_PAYMENT_RETRY state and pauses up to 5 minutes.
Send this signal to wake it and retry payment immediately.
Has no effect if the workflow is not waiting for retry.

item.name: C. Force Payment Failure (set failure-rate=1.0 first)

request.description:
Set SIMULATION_PAYMENT_FAILURE_RATE=1.0 in docker-compose.yml and restart payment-service, then add items and confirm this order.
Temporal retries 3× (all fail with InsufficientFunds — non-retryable), then compensation runs:
ReleaseInventory → OrderCancelled.

Send cancel while currentStep=PROCESSING_PAYMENT (during the 10s payment activity window).
The signal is queued; the workflow checks it when the activity returns.

Compensation sequence: recordPaymentCompleted → refundPayment → releaseInventory → recordOrderCancelled.
Result: status=CANCELLED, paymentStatus=REFUNDED.

item.name: 🌐 Kong Gateway Routes
item.description:
Order-service requests routed through Kong API Gateway (port 8000).

Kong configuration (kong-init.sh):
- Route: paths[]=/orders → service: order-service (http://order-service:8080)
- strip_path=false — /orders prefix is preserved when forwarding
- Plugin: rate-limiting (60 req/min, 1000 req/hr, policy=local)
- Plugin: correlation-id (X-Correlation-Id header injected on every request)

Coverage: ALL /orders/* endpoints are routed through Kong.
NOT routed: /workflows/* endpoints — call order-service directly on port 8080.

Response headers added by Kong:
- X-Correlation-Id: auto-generated UUID (echoed back to client)
- X-RateLimit-Remaining-Minute: remaining requests this minute
- X-RateLimit-Remaining-Hour: remaining requests this hour

request.description:
Creates a DRAFT order through Kong.
Response includes X-Correlation-Id (injected by Kong correlation-id plugin) and rate-limit headers.
Removes an item from a DRAFT order. productId in path must match an added item.

exec: console.log('Status:', json.status, '| Payment:', json.paymentStatus, '| Shipment:', json.shipmentStatus);

request.description: Returns raw domain events from the event_store table. Routed through Kong.
Human-readable event timeline. Routed through Kong.

Confirms order and starts Temporal saga. Returns workflowId.

Note: the subsequent GET /workflows/{id} calls must go to port 8080 directly — /workflows is not a Kong route.

Sends cancel signal to Temporal workflow (or directly cancels a DRAFT order) via Kong.
Compensation runs asynchronously inside Temporal.

Sends RetryPayment signal via Kong. Wakes a workflow paused in WAITING_FOR_PAYMENT_RETRY.
Order-service translates this to a Temporal signal internally.
